description_long: This is a round, pavilion-style tent with alternating red and white vertical stripes on its fabric canopy, reminiscent of a circus or festival tent. The tent features a peaked roof and scalloped valance around the edge, giving it a decorative and inviting appearance. At the front, there is a wide entrance opening supported by two visible wooden poles, with a small awning or counter extending outward, possibly for serving or ticketing purposes. The tent's structure appears to be supported by internal poles, with the fabric stretched taut over the frame. The side and rear views show the tent as fully enclosed, with no additional openings or windows visible. The overall design suggests it is intended for temporary outdoor use in parks, fairs, or urban events.
description: A round, red-and-white striped pavilion tent with a peaked roof, scalloped valance, and a front entrance supported by poles and a small awning or counter.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, two supporting poles, and a small awning or counter extending outward.
description_view_1: Angled side view revealing the entrance and the awning/counter protruding from the left side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the fully enclosed back of the tent with no visible openings or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the awning/counter extending from the right, with the entrance not visible.
attribute_entrance_type: Flap entrance at the front, supported by two wooden poles and covered by a small awning/counter.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No guy lines are visible; tent stability appears to rely on internal structure and two front support poles.
